summary: Russia launched a massive combined missile and jet-powered drone attack on Kyiv overnight July 2, killing 13 and injuring 86, while simultaneous strikes hit Zaporizhzhia, Kharkiv, Pavlohrad, and Sumy regions. Ukrainian air defenses intercepted drones over Moscow, Leningrad, Rostov, and Crimea regions, where blackouts occurred. Ground fighting intensified across eastern and northern fronts with Russian forces advancing near Kostyantynivka, Dobropillya, and in Sumy and Kharkiv directions.
key_points: Russia fired over 50 missiles and 70+ jet-powered Shahed drones at Kyiv from Chernihiv and Bryansk directions, causing 13 deaths and 86 injuries. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68493)
Ukrainian air defenses shot down 7 UAVs over Leningrad region, 5 near Moscow, and 25+ in Rostov region overnight. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315)
Russian assault troops continue small-arms battles in Sumy region settlements including Bachevsk, Pisarevka, and Novaya Sech. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315)
Russian forces liberated Ukrainske in Vovchansk district and press Kazachya Lopan, Losevka cemetery area, and Petro-Ivanovka. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315)
OMON explosives technician killed and police officer wounded defusing Ukrainian drone warhead in Rylsk, Kursk region. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315)
Belgorod region settlements under drone strikes: Malakeyevo (1 killed, 1 wounded), Zenino, Borisovka, Shebekino, Volokonovka casualties reported. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315)
DeepStateUA reports 84 sq km net territorial gain for Russia in June, with assault actions up 4.4% and advances near Kostyantynivka, DKU, Huliaipole. (Source: @DeepStateUA/23612)
EU's Kallas proposes new sanctions on Russian defense industry companies after Kyiv attack; Ukraine's Sybiha endorses proactive deterrence approach. (Source: @UkraineNow/82335)

sections.executive_summary: Russia executed a massive overnight barrage on Kyiv using over 50 missiles and 70+ jet-powered Shahed drones launched from Chernihiv and Bryansk regions, killing 13 civilians and injuring 86 while damaging over 20 locations including residential buildings, a scientific institute, and industrial facilities. Simultaneous strikes hit Zaporizhzhia, Kharkiv, Pavlohrad, and Sumy regions, while Ukrainian air defenses intercepted drones over Moscow, Leningrad, Rostov, and Crimea regions where blackouts were reported. Ground combat intensified across multiple axes with Russian forces conducting assault operations in Sumy, Kharkiv, Kupyansk, Krasny Liman, Konstantinovka, and Dobropillya directions, achieving territorial gains near Kostyantynivka and Huliaipole per DeepStateUA mapping.

points: Kyiv suffered 13 fatalities and 86 injuries (70 hospitalized) from massive combined missile and jet-drone attack; over 20 locations damaged including residential buildings, a high-speed rail station, scientific institute, hotel, and enterprises. (Source: @UkraineNow/82328)
Industrial enterprise in Bucha district burned down due to aerial attack during the overnight barrage. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31322)

Ombudsman Lubinets demands UN Red Cross enforce 'silence regime' July 3, 9:00-18:00 for humanitarian evacuation of 6,000 civilians (including 200 children) from Oleshky, Kherson region. (Source: @UkraineNow/82321)
77% of Ukrainians disapprove of Territorial Recruitment Centers (TCC) per June 2026 Rating Group poll: 53% strongly disapprove, 24% somewhat disapprove. (Source: @UkraineNow/82324)

National Bank of Ukraine pursues dismissal of Ukrposhta CEO Smilyanskyi, considering direct shareholder demand, payment license revocation, or legislative fixes after Cabinet ignored initial demand. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31299)
Rada deputies voted to create national pantheon for reburial of UNR, ZUNR, and OUN-UPA nationalist leaders as 'space of national memory'. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31296)

Russian forces launched massive strike on Kyiv with air- and sea-launched missiles plus jet-powered strike UAVs including Shaheds, complicating air defense; Zelensky warned publicly in advance; gas stations closed demonstratively. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315)
Russian air defenses intercepted 7 Ukrainian UAVs over Leningrad region and 5 on approach to Moscow overnight. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315)
Crimea power supply complicated by raids: blackouts in Feodosia and Armyansk, repair work in Saki reported by regional authorities. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315)
Rostov region air defenses shot down more than 25 drones overnight. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315)

In Rylsk, Kursk region: 6 wounded from AFU strikes; Ukrainian UAVs scattered explosive devices on two squares; OMON explosives technician killed and police officer wounded during warhead defusal. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315)

Belgorod region drone strikes: Malakeyevo (man killed, wife wounded), Zenino (Orlan unit fighter injured), Borisovka (man and woman with shrapnel wounds), Shebekino (man with barotrauma), Volokonovka (man wounded at enterprise). (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315)
Fighting continues for Krasny Liman and surroundings; Russian MoD counting houses occupied by assault troops. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315)
Heavy offensive battles ongoing in northern part of Konstantinovka; Russian forces conducting sustained attacks. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315)
Combat on eastern outskirts of Dobropillya; enemy aims to contain Russian forces while building next defensive echelon. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315)
DeepStateUA reports Russian advances in June near Kostyantynivka, DKU, and Huliaipole contributing to 84 sq km net territorial gain. (Source: @DeepStateUA/23612)
Assault actions increased 4.4% in June; many attacks involve single infantrymen, motivation questioned. (Source: @DeepStateUA/23612)

Kupyansk direction: repulsion of AFU counterattack using armored vehicles reported; Russian small assault groups deepening into Kupyansk-Uzlovoy urban areas; front leveling near Podoly. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315)

Sumy direction: Russian 'North' group assault troops continue small-arms battles in Bachevsk and surroundings (Shostka district); fighting in Pisarevka, Marino, Novaya Sech, Khoten (Sumy district); forest battles in Krasnopollye district. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315)
Sumy region residents report most apartment buildings without electricity since June 30; water supply absent in many homes after intensified Russian strikes. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315)

Kharkiv direction: 'North' group offensive operations in Kazachya Lopan and adjacent forests; fighting at Losevka cemetery area and Zemlyanoy Yar (Vovchansk sector); Ukrainske in Vovchansk district liberated. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315)
Velyky Burluk sector: small-arms battles in Petro-Ivanovka and surroundings, forest areas near Budarky and Zemlyanky. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315)

Jet-powered Shahed drones tracked from Bryansk region into Chernihiv region toward Kyiv; multiple drones from Chernihiv region into Kyiv region (Vyshhorod, Brovary districts, near Kyiv reservoir). (Source: @eRadarrua/99322, @mon1tor_ua/68495, @kpszsu/67103)

Enemy UAVs detected heading toward Kyiv from north (Krasyatychi) and east of Kyiv reservoir; drones toward Sumy from north; drones toward Kharkiv from north. (Source: @kpszsu/67115, @kpszsu/67113, @kpszsu/67106)
Zaporizhzhia targeted by KAB guided bombs and UAVs from western direction; multiple drone alerts issued. (Source: @kpszsu/67107, @kpszsu/67113, @kpszsu/67103)
Dnipropetrovsk region (Kryvyi Rih) under UAV threat from southern direction. (Source: @kpszsu/67115)

Kherson region: Oleshky and surrounding villages hold estimated 6,000 civilians including 200 children; Ukraine demands humanitarian corridor and return of Oleshky boarding school children. (Source: @UkraineNow/82321)
Crimea experiencing power supply complications: blackouts in Feodosia and Armyansk, repairs in Saki after raids. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315)
Vinnytsia region: drone alert for Sharhorod. (Source: @eRadarrua/99325)
Cherkasy region reported under Russian strikes overnight. (Source: @UkraineNow/82328)

Massive overnight attack on Kyiv: over 50 missiles and 70+ jet-powered Shahed drones launched from Chernihiv and Bryansk regions; approximate routes mapped. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68493, @mon1tor_ua/68481, @mon1tor_ua/68461)

Ukrainian observer Bogdan Miroshnikov reports ~60 missiles of various types (Iskander ballistic, S-400 missiles, Zircons) and 100+ jet drones in single salvo, complicating air defense. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31313)
Kyiv air raid alerts active 10:48 and 11:48; all-clear at 11:20 on July 2. (Source: @eRadarrua/99330, @eRadarrua/99347, @eRadarrua/99339)

Jet-powered Shaheds tracked: from Chernihiv to Kyiv (Vyshhorod, Brovary districts); from Bryansk to Chernihiv; 4 jets past Slavutych toward Kyiv reservoir. (Source: @eRadarrua/99325, @eRadarrua/99322, @mon1tor_ua/68495)
Russian air defenses: 7 UAVs downed over Leningrad region, 5 near Moscow, 25+ in Rostov region. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315)
Ukrainian drone strikes: Rylsk (explosive devices on squares), Belgorod region multiple settlements, Kursk region (6 wounded). (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315)

key_developments: Russia launched largest combined missile/jet-drone attack on Kyiv of war: 50+ missiles, 70+ jet Shaheds from Chernihiv/Bryansk, 13 killed, 86 injured, 20+ sites hit including residential, industrial, infrastructure. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68493)
Ukrainian air defense intercepted drones deep inside Russia: 7 over Leningrad region, 5 near Moscow, 25+ in Rostov region, indicating extended reach. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315)

Russian 'North' group conducting simultaneous assault operations across Sumy (Bachevsk, Pisarevka, Novaya Sech) and Kharkiv (Kazachya Lopan, Vovchansk, Ukrainske liberated) axes. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315)

Russian forces advancing in Donetsk region: heavy battles in northern Konstantinovka, eastern Dobropillya, Krasny Liman; DeepStateUA confirms 84 sq km net gain in June near Kostyantynivka, DKU, Huliaipole. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315, @DeepStateUA/23612)
Belgorod and Kursk border regions under sustained Ukrainian drone strikes: multiple casualties, infrastructure hits, OMON technician killed defusing warhead in Rylsk. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315)
EU's Kallas proposes expanded sanctions on Russian defense industry companies post-Kyiv attack; Ukraine's Sybiha calls for proactive deterrence. (Source: @UkraineNow/82335)
Humanitarian crisis in Kherson region: 6,000 civilians trapped in Oleshky; Lubinets demands July 3 silence regime 9:00-18:00 for evacuation via Red Cross. (Source: @UkraineNow/82321)
sections.threat_assessment: The July 2 barrage demonstrates Russia's growing capacity to saturate Ukrainian air defenses with combined missile and jet-powered drone salvos, leveraging launch positions in Chernihiv and Bryansk regions to reduce flight times. Simultaneous pressure across Sumy, Kharkiv, Kupyansk, and Donetsk axes suggests a coordinated campaign to fix Ukrainian reserves while exploiting localized advantages near Kostyantynivka and Huliaipole. Ukrainian strikes on Russian border regions (Belgorod, Kursk) and deep rear (Leningrad, Moscow, Rostov) indicate expanding asymmetric capabilities but have not yet disrupted Russian offensive tempo. The humanitarian situation in Oleshky reflects Russia's continued disregard for civilian protection norms, likely complicating Ukrainian operational planning in the southern axis.
sections.regional_response: EU High Representative Kallas's proposal for expanded sanctions targeting Russian defense industry supporters marks a shift toward proactive economic measures tied directly to specific attacks, though implementation timelines remain uncertain. Ukraine's diplomatic corps (Sybiha) is leveraging the Kyiv strike to advocate for preemptive deterrence frameworks rather than reactive responses. Domestic pressure mounts on Ukrainian authorities with 77% TCC disapproval and mobilization controversies (Kryvyi Rih case), potentially affecting force generation. Russia's internal security apparatus faces strain from cross-border drone attacks causing casualties and infrastructure damage in Belgorod, Kursk, and deeper regions, possibly diverting air defense assets from frontline sectors.
sections.intelligence_notes: The proliferation of jet-powered Shahed variants launched from northern axes (Chernihiv, Bryansk) reduces Ukrainian reaction time and complicates interception geometry, suggesting Russian adaptation to air defense improvements. DeepStateUA's assessment of 84 sq km net Russian gain in June with rising assault frequency but low quality (single-infantry attacks) indicates a war of attrition favoring mass over maneuver. The OMON technician death in Rylsk during UAV warhead defusal highlights evolving Ukrainian drone payload tactics (scattering explosive devices). Crimea power disruptions (Feodosia, Armyansk) and Rostov drone interceptions (25+) reveal vulnerability of Russian rear logistics and energy infrastructure to Ukrainian long-range strikes. Monitoring of Kupyansk-Uzlovoy urban penetration by Russian assault groups will indicate whether this becomes a new vector for operational envelopment.
